events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a residential electrical job, specifically Data/comm & security work at the rough-complete stage. The observed field condition is "bend radius violations." References are TIA-568 and NEC 800.

Bend radius violations in data/comm cabling (like Cat5e/Cat6) can cause crosstalk, return loss, and signal degradation. TIA-568 specifies minimum bend radius requirements. NEC 800 covers communications circuits.

This is a defect - the cable is installed but with bend radius violations, making it installed-defective and non-compliant.

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: medium (signal degradation, potential need for rework)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: rough stage, maybe 60-70%

Let me construct the JSON.

```json
  "findings": [
    "Data/comm cable installed with bend radius tighter than manufacturer/TIA-568 minimum, causing kinking or sharp angles at terminations and bends",
    "Excessive bend radius can impair pair twist geometry, increasing crosstalk, return loss, and attenuation; may fail certification testing",
    "Cable not compliant with NEC 800 / TIA-568 handling and installation requirements for communications cabling"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-pull or re-route affected cable runs to maintain manufacturer-specified minimum bend radius (typically 4x cable OD for UTP, 10x for some shielded/fiber)",
    "Replace any cable with visible kinks, crushed sections, or jacket deformation from tight bends",
    "Use proper bend-radius-forming fittings at boxes, studs, and termination points; support cable without stressing bends",
    "Perform certification/continuity testing after correction before closing walls"
  ],
  "rationale": "At rough-complete, data/comm cabling shows bend radius violations. TIA-568 and manufacturer instructions prescribe minimum bend radii to preserve signal performance; violating them creates an installed-defective, non-compliant condition likely to fail certification and require rework before closeout.",
  "references": ["TIA-568", "NEC 800"]
}
```
